compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: DECISION: item 8 (module-size allowlist governance) REJECTED. Requiring human review on the allowlist adds too much friction for a soft code-quality gate. Chosen approach: the allowlist stays a RECORD, remediated periodically through maintenance sessions (like this audit). No ratchet / CODEOWNERS-gate / merge-base delta check. Item 9 (import-cycle SCC) APPROVED — all 3 sub-remediations.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Pushed the 13-commit remediation stack to Gerrit (changes 202-214), all with Change-Id + rebar-ticket trailers, rebased onto current origin/main. Full suite green (4267 passed; the 1 e2e editor failure is a pre-existing worktree-env issue — missing editor_assets/node_modules — verified identical at baseline). Merged already: 186/187/188 incl. the Critical has_llm_steps fix. 189 (child-closure docs) to re-push after the stack merges (Fix B/change 212 adds the .gitleaks.toml that clears its false-positive). Items 13/14/15/9.3 (config/reconcile splits, shared-helper sweep, dir reorg, facade reroute) CHECKPOINTED for a post-release maintenance pass.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: MILESTONE: all 13 remediation changes (202-214) MERGED to main via Gerrit (two-bot gate: LLM-Review + Verified/CI), plus the earlier Critical has_llm_steps fix (188). One CI hiccup handled mid-flight: change 211 (Fix A) failed the mypy gate — a type-var issue in the new blocking-finding code (my gap: ran make lint but not make typecheck on the batch); fixed by str-coercing the file list, amended into 211, re-verified (mypy 246 files clean), re-pushed, merged. Change 189 (child-closure docs) re-pushed onto main-with-.gitleaks.toml: its LLM-Review block CLEARED (now +1) — end-to-end validation that the Fix A rendering bug + Fix B gitleaks allowlist resolved the false -1/0-findings vote. 189 CI finishing. Remaining epic children: 13/14/15/9.3 (config/reconcile splits, shared-helper sweep, dir reorg, facade reroute) — CHECKPOINTED for a post-release maintenance pass.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: EPIC STATUS after this session: MERGED to main — the Critical has_llm_steps gate-bypass fix (188), the full 13-change remediation stack (202-214: bugs, gate hardening, reducer inversion, import-cycle guard, decompositions, dead code, the two 189 review-bot fixes), item 13 (config/reconcile/editor/batch_dispatch splits, 218-221), and item 14 (run_git/atomic_write/_reap/_lazy_load helpers, 225-228). ALL those child tickets are CLOSED. REMAINING (2): (a) item 9 part 3 / 9.3 facade-read reroute under ebd8 (lofty-flare-crest); (b) item 15 directory-reorg + TicketQuery under 6ae5 (toll-clock-tier). Both checkpointed for a post-release pass. Item 8 (allowlist governance) was REJECTED earlier (allowlist stays a record). Epic stays OPEN until 9.3 + item 15 land.

compiled_state.description:
# Pre-release codebase-health remediations (2026-07-02 joe-janitor audit)

## Context

A verified codebase-health audit (joe-janitor, 2026-07-02: 8 concern agents + temporal
pass, then 4 adversarial verification agents; 0 findings refuted) ran ahead of next week's
wider release. Full report at `.joe-janitor/report-2026-07-02.md`. Overall health is good and
improved since the 2026-06-24 audit, but a Critical gate-bypass, several High structural/governance
items, and a set of Mediums warrant remediation before increased external scrutiny.

This epic is the umbrella for the remediation work. Children are created per finding as they are
approved; the Critical (risk 1) and the completion-gate doc drift (risk 2) are remediated first.

## Success Criteria

- The Critical `ALLOW_LLM` gate bypass is fixed with a regression test; both shipped gate
  workflows are detected by `has_llm_steps`.
- Completion-gate docs match the post-`30c2ef3ad` child-closure semantics (blocking vs
  certification-withholding); the "unsigned close ⇒ validation failed" biconditional is corrected.
- Each approved remediation lands as its own child through Gerrit (LLM-Review + Verified),
  with a `rebar-ticket:` trailer, plan-review at claim, and a signed completion verdict at close.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Risk 1 (has_llm_steps ALLOW_LLM bypass) remediated + regression test; child closed.
- [ ] Risk 2 (completion-gate child-closure doc drift) remediated; child closed.
- [ ] Stale-reference doc/comment fixes (already landed) tracked under a child.
- [ ] The remaining approved remediations are decomposed into children, each with AC + file-impact.
- [ ] All children closed with signed completion verdicts; report's Critical/High set addressed.
compiled_state.title: Pre-release codebase-health remediations (2026-07-02 audit)
